_comment: Hover-tooltip definitions for the book grouping/status chips. Keys must match the chip labels exactly (see src/lib/canon.ts). Edit freely; the build reads this file as-is.
Pentateuch: The first five books of the Old Testament (Genesis through Deuteronomy), traditionally ascribed to Moses.
Law: The books of the Law (Hebrew: Torah) — the five Mosaic books setting out God's covenant and commandments to Israel.
Torah: Hebrew for 'instruction' or 'law'; the five books of Moses, Genesis through Deuteronomy.
History: The historical books, recounting Israel's story from the conquest of Canaan through the post-exilic and Maccabean eras.
Prophecy: Books counted among the prophets (Hebrew: Nevi'im) — from the 'Former Prophets' (Joshua–Kingdoms) to the major and minor prophetic books.
Former Prophet: The 'Former Prophets' (Hebrew: Nevi'im Rishonim) of the Hebrew canon — Joshua, Judges, and the four Kingdoms (Samuel–Kings) — narrating Israel's history through a prophetic lens.
Latter Prophet: The 'Latter Prophets' (Hebrew: Nevi'im Aharonim) — the major prophetic books of Isaiah, Jeremiah, and Ezekiel.
12 Prophets: The Twelve (the 'Minor Prophets,' Hebrew: Tere Asar), counted as a single book in the Hebrew and Greek tradition — Hosea through Malachi.
Writing: The 'Writings' (Hebrew: Ketuvim) — the third division of the Hebrew Bible, gathering poetry, wisdom, and later historical books.
Poetic: The poetic and wisdom books — Psalms, Proverbs, Job, and the wisdom literature.
5 Scrolls (Megillot): The Five Scrolls (Hebrew: Megillot) read at Jewish festivals — Song of Songs, Ruth, Lamentations, Ecclesiastes, and Esther.
Gospel: An account of the life, death, and resurrection of Jesus Christ — from the Holy Apostles and Evangelists Matthew, Mark, Luke, and John. Greek for 'good news.'
Letter / Epistle: A letter written to a church or an individual; the epistles make up much of the New Testament.
Pauline: Letters attributed to the Holy Apostle Paul.
General (Catholic): The 'catholic' (universal) epistles, addressed to the Church at large rather than to one congregation — James, Peter, John, Jude (and Hebrews here).
Petrine: Letters attributed to the Holy Apostle Peter — 1 and 2 Peter.
Johannine: Books associated with the Holy Apostle and Evangelist John the Theologian — his Gospel, the three Johannine epistles, and Revelation.
Anagignoskomenon: 'To be read' — a book of the wider Orthodox Old Testament received for reading and edification, beyond the shorter Hebrew canon (the Anagignoskomena / Readables).
Homologoumenon: 'Acknowledged' — a New Testament book accepted as canonical without dispute from early times.
Antilegomenon: 'Spoken against' — a New Testament book whose canonicity was disputed in the early Church before being received.
